-- Function to compute a quarter with an arbitrary start month (1..12)
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION what_quarter_is(dt date, start_month int DEFAULT 1)
RETURNS int
LANGUAGE SQL
IMMUTABLE
STRICT
AS $$
    SELECT (( (extract(month from dt)::int - start_month + 12) % 12) / 3) + 1
$$;

-- Examples:
-- Feb 16, 2001 with April start => Q4
SELECT what_quarter_is((TIMESTAMP '2001-02-16 20:38:40')::date, 4);

-- Jul 23, 1995 with April start => Q2
SELECT what_quarter_is(DATE '1995-07-23', 4);

WITH months AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT
         user_id,
         to_date(purchase_date || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS month_date
  FROM purchase
),
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    user_id,
    month_date,
    row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY month_date) AS rn
  FROM months
),
islands AS (
  SELECT
    user_id,
    ((extract(year FROM month_date)::int * 12) + extract(month FROM month_date)::int) - rn AS grp
  FROM ordered
),
streaks AS (
  SELECT user_id, grp, count(*) AS streak_len
  FROM islands
  GROUP BY user_id, grp
),
longest AS (
  SELECT user_id, max(streak_len) AS longest_streak
  FROM streaks
  GROUP BY user_id
)
SELECT longest_streak AS streak_length,
       count(*) AS user_count
FROM longest
GROUP BY longest_streak
ORDER BY streak_length DESC;

WITH team_goals AS (
  SELECT
    m.home_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    m.home_team_goal AS goals_scored,
    m.away_team_goal AS goals_conceded,
    (m."date")::timestamp AS match_ts
  FROM "match" m
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    m.away_team_api_id AS team_api_id,
    m.away_team_goal AS goals_scored,
    m.home_team_goal AS goals_conceded,
    (m."date")::timestamp AS match_ts
  FROM "match" m
)
SELECT
  tg.team_api_id,
  EXTRACT(HOUR FROM tg.match_ts) AS match_hour,
  AVG(tg.goals_scored) AS avg_goals_scored,
  AVG(tg.goals_conceded) AS avg_goals_conceded,
  SUM(tg.goals_scored) AS total_goals_scored,
  SUM(tg.goals_conceded) AS total_goals_conceded,
  MAX(tg.goals_scored) AS max_goals_scored,
  MIN(tg.goals_scored) AS min_goals_scored,
  COUNT(*) AS match_count
FROM team_goals tg
GROUP BY tg.team_api_id, EXTRACT(HOUR FROM tg.match_ts)
ORDER BY tg.team_api_id, match_hour;

-- Add the target column
ALTER TABLE "trans" ADD COLUMN next_bank text;

-- Populate next_bank with the next non-null bank within each account ordered by date (and trans_id to break ties)
WITH next_vals AS (
  SELECT
    t.trans_id,
    (
      ARRAY_AGG(t.bank) FILTER (WHERE t.bank IS NOT NULL)
      OVER (
        PARTITION BY t.account_id
        ORDER BY t.date, t.trans_id
        ROWS BETWEEN 1 FOLLOWING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
      )
    )[1] AS next_bank
  FROM "trans" t
)
UPDATE "trans" AS t
SET next_bank = nv.next_bank
FROM next_vals AS nv
WHERE t.trans_id = nv.trans_id;

WITH atoms_by_mol AS (
  SELECT a.molecule_id,
         json_agg(
           json_build_object(
             'atom_id', a.atom_id,
             'element', a.element
           )
           ORDER BY a.atom_id
         ) AS atoms
  FROM atom a
  GROUP BY a.molecule_id
),
bonds_by_mol AS (
  SELECT b.molecule_id,
         json_agg(
           json_build_object(
             'bond_id', b.bond_id,
             'bond_type', b.bond_type,
             'atom_id', c.atom_id,
             'atom_id2', c.atom_id2
           )
           ORDER BY b.bond_id
         ) AS bonds
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT ON (bond_id) bond_id, atom_id, atom_id2
    FROM connected
    ORDER BY bond_id, atom_id
  ) c
  JOIN bond b ON b.bond_id = c.bond_id
  GROUP BY b.molecule_id
),
molecules_by_label AS (
  SELECT m.label,
         json_agg(
           json_build_object(
             'molecule_id', m.molecule_id,
             'atoms', COALESCE(a.atoms, '[]'::json),
             'bonds', COALESCE(b.bonds, '[]'::json)
           )
           ORDER BY m.molecule_id
         ) AS molecules
  FROM molecule m
  LEFT JOIN atoms_by_mol a ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
  LEFT JOIN bonds_by_mol b ON b.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
  GROUP BY m.label
)
SELECT json_object_agg(label, molecules) AS molecules_by_label
FROM molecules_by_label;

WITH race_counts AS (

    s.gender_id,
    s.race_id,
    COUNT(*)::bigint AS cnt
  FROM superhero AS s
  GROUP BY s.gender_id, s.race_id
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    gender_id,
    race_id,
    cnt,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt DESC) AS top_rank,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY gender_id ORDER BY cnt ASC)  AS bottom_rank
  FROM race_counts
)
-- Return both top-5 and bottom-5 per gender_id (including ties)
SELECT
  r.gender_id,
  r.race_id,
  r.cnt,
  r.top_rank AS rank,
  'top' AS which
FROM ranked r
WHERE r.top_rank <= 5
UNION ALL
SELECT
  r.gender_id,
  r.race_id,
  r.cnt,
  r.bottom_rank AS rank,
  'bottom' AS which
FROM ranked r
WHERE r.bottom_rank <= 5
ORDER BY gender_id, which, rank, cnt DESC, race_id;

  SELECT

  FROM budget
  WHERE LOWER(category) IN ('attendees', 'attendance', 'attendance count', 'attendee count')

  ON a.link_to_event = b.link_to_event
WHERE COALESCE(a.attendee_count, -1) <> COALESCE(b.attendee_count_in_budget, -1);

-- 1) Helpful covering index so the grouping/join is fast
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS transaction_info_cust_amt_type_trx_idx
  ON transaction_info (customerid, amount, "type", transaction);

-- 2) Single set-based update (handles NULLs correctly and only writes changed rows)
UPDATE transaction_info t
SET first_transaction = ft.min_trx::text
FROM (
  SELECT customerid, amount, "type", MIN(transaction) AS min_trx
  FROM transaction_info
  GROUP BY customerid, amount, "type"
) ft
WHERE t.customerid = ft.customerid
  AND t.amount IS NOT DISTINCT FROM ft.amount
  AND t."type" IS NOT DISTINCT FROM ft."type"
  AND (t.first_transaction IS DISTINCT FROM ft.min_trx::text);

WITH first_purchase AS (
  SELECT
    customer_id,
    MIN(to_date(month_year || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD')) AS first_month
  FROM orders
  GROUP BY customer_id
),
orders_parsed AS (
  SELECT
    o.customer_id,
    o.month_year,
    to_date(o.month_year || '-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD') AS month_dt
  FROM orders o
)
SELECT
  op.month_year,
  COUNT(DISTINCT op.customer_id) AS repeat_customer_count
FROM orders_parsed op
JOIN first_purchase fp
  ON op.customer_id = fp.customer_id
WHERE op.month_dt > fp.first_month
GROUP BY op.month_year
ORDER BY op.month_year;

WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT
    ctid AS row_id,
    player_id,
    regexp_split_to_array(stats_keys, '\s*,\s*') AS key_arr,
    regexp_split_to_array(stats_values, '\s*,\s*') AS val_arr
  FROM player_stats
),
kv AS (
  SELECT
    p.row_id,
    p.player_id,
    k.key_elem AS metric,
    v.val_elem AS metric_value
  FROM parsed p
  JOIN LATERAL unnest(p.key_arr) WITH ORDINALITY AS k(key_elem, ord) ON true
  JOIN LATERAL unnest(p.val_arr) WITH ORDINALITY AS v(val_elem, ord) ON v.ord = k.ord
)
SELECT
  player_id,
  COALESCE(MAX((metric_value)::int) FILTER (WHERE metric = 'goals'), 0) AS goals,
  COALESCE(MAX((metric_value)::int) FILTER (WHERE metric = 'assists'), 0) AS assists,
  COALESCE(MAX((metric_value)::int) FILTER (WHERE metric = 'yellow_cards'), 0) AS yellow_cards
FROM kv
GROUP BY row_id, player_id
ORDER BY player_id;

    ct.amount,
    SIGN(ct.amount) AS s,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ct.club_name ORDER BY ct.transaction_date)
      - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ct.club_name, SIGN(ct.amount) ORDER BY ct.transaction_date) AS grp
  FROM club_transactions ct
  WHERE ct.amount <> 0
)

  club_name,
  CASE s WHEN 1 THEN 'positive' WHEN -1 THEN 'negative' ELSE 'zero' END AS sign,
  MIN(transaction_date) AS start_date,

SELECT
  base.relname AS base_table,
  base_col.attname AS base_col,
  ref.relname AS referencing_table,
  ref_col.attname AS referencing_col,
  'CONSTRAINT ' || quote_ident(c.conname) || ' ' || pg_get_constraintdef(c.oid) AS constraint_sql
FROM pg_constraint c
JOIN pg_class base ON base.oid = c.confrelid
JOIN pg_class ref  ON ref.oid  = c.conrelid
-- pair up referenced (confkey) and referencing (conkey) columns by position
JOIN LATERAL unnest(c.confkey) WITH ORDINALITY AS rk(attnum, ord) ON TRUE
JOIN LATERAL unnest(c.conkey)  WITH ORDINALITY AS fk(attnum, ord) ON fk.ord = rk.ord
JOIN pg_attribute base_col ON base_col.attrelid = base.oid AND base_col.attnum = rk.attnum
JOIN pg_attribute ref_col  ON ref_col.attrelid  = ref.oid  AND ref_col.attnum  = fk.attnum
WHERE c.contype = 'f'
  AND base.relname = 'users'
ORDER BY referencing_table, c.conname, rk.ord;

WITH logins AS (
  SELECT
    user_id,
    action_time AS login_time,
    LEAD(action_time) OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY action_time) AS next_login_time
  FROM user_actions
  WHERE action = 'LOGIN'
)
SELECT
  s.user_id,
  s.action_time AS abandoned_search_time
FROM user_actions AS s
JOIN logins AS l
  ON l.user_id = s.user_id
 AND s.action_time >= l.login_time
 AND s.action_time < l.next_login_time
WHERE s.action = 'SEARCH'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM user_actions AS b
    WHERE b.user_id = s.user_id
      AND b.action = 'BUY'
      AND b.action_time > s.action_time
      AND b.action_time < l.next_login_time
  )
ORDER BY s.user_id, s.action_time;

select
  driverid,
  points,
  -- lead needs a deterministic order; include a tiebreaker
  lead(driverid) over (order by points asc, driverid asc) as "lead(driverId) with order",
  -- With ORDER BY in the window, the default frame is "unbounded preceding to current row"
  -- which makes this a running aggregation. To aggregate the full partition (like the no-ORDER case),
  -- specify the frame explicitly:
  array_agg(driverid) over (
    order by points asc, driverid asc
    rows between unbounded preceding and unbounded following
  ) as "array_agg(driverId) with order",
  -- A LEAD/LAG without ORDER BY is nondeterministic; use a stable order for comparison
  lead(driverid) over (order by driverid asc) as "lead(driverId) (by driverId)",
  -- No ORDER BY in the window means the frame is the whole partition by default.
  -- The order of elements inside the array is unspecified unless you ORDER BY.
  array_agg(driverid) over (
    rows between unbounded preceding and unbounded following
  ) as "array_agg(driverId) without order"
from results
where raceid = 19
order by driverid asc;

    END AS is_break
  FROM superhero_missions
),
grp AS (

      PARTITION BY hero_id

WITH RECURSIVE path_cte AS (
  -- start from heads (no previous card points to them)
  SELECT c.id, c.nextcardid, c.id::text AS path, c.id AS start_id
  FROM collection c
  LEFT JOIN collection prev ON prev.nextcardid = c.id
  WHERE prev.id IS NULL

  UNION ALL

  -- walk forward until we reach tails (nextcardid IS NULL)
  SELECT n.id, n.nextcardid, path_cte.path || ' --> ' || n.id, path_cte.start_id
  FROM path_cte
  JOIN collection n ON n.id = path_cte.nextcardid
)
SELECT path
FROM path_cte
WHERE nextcardid IS NULL
ORDER BY start_id;

JOIN route_detail de
  ON de.route_id = r.id
WHERE de.visit_status = 5
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM route_event ev
    WHERE ev.route_detail_id = de.id
      AND ev.event_type = 3
  )
ORDER BY r.id, de.id;

SELECT
  m.molecule_id,
  COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN a.element = 'na' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) AS na_atoms,
  CASE
    WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN a.element IN ('c', 'cl') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN 1
    ELSE 0
  END AS c_or_cl_atoms
FROM molecule m
LEFT JOIN atom a
  ON a.molecule_id = m.molecule_id
GROUP BY m.molecule_id;

-- Update if exists; otherwise insert. This avoids consuming a new loan_id on update.
WITH updated AS (
  UPDATE loan
  SET amount = amount + 1000
  WHERE account_id = 2
    AND date = DATE '1996-04-29'
  RETURNING 1
)
INSERT INTO loan (account_id, date, amount, duration, payments, status)
SELECT 2, DATE '1996-04-29', 30276, 12, 2523.0, 'B'
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM updated);

-- Parent if parent.score >= 20
-- Else accepted child if parent.score < 20 and child.score >= 20
SELECT q.id, q.acceptedanswerid, q.posttypeid, q.score
FROM posts AS q
WHERE q.posttypeid = 1
  AND q.score >= 20

UNION ALL

SELECT a.id, a.acceptedanswerid, a.posttypeid, a.score
FROM posts AS q
JOIN posts AS a
  ON a.id = q.acceptedanswerid
WHERE q.posttypeid = 1
  AND q.score < 20
  AND a.score >= 20;

SELECT
  a.hero_id,
  a.attribute_id,
  COUNT(js.value) AS ss_cnt,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE js.value->>'approved' = 'true') AS approved_cnt
FROM hero_attribute AS a
LEFT JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(a.ss) AS js(value) ON true
WHERE a.hero_id IN (1, 2)
GROUP BY a.hero_id, a.attribute_id;

-- Fastest and simplest (assumes all five GK fields are non-null)
SELECT
  id,
  (
    (gk_diving + gk_handling + gk_kicking + gk_positioning + gk_reflexes)
    - GREATEST(gk_diving, gk_handling, gk_kicking, gk_positioning, gk_reflexes)
    - LEAST(gk_diving, gk_handling, gk_kicking, gk_positioning, gk_reflexes)
  ) / 3.0 AS adjusted_average
FROM player_attributes
ORDER BY id;

-- Null-safe version (ignores nulls; returns NULL if fewer than 3 non-null values)
SELECT
  pa.id,
  CASE
    WHEN stats.cnt > 2 THEN (stats.sum - stats.max - stats.min) / (stats.cnt - 2.0)
    ELSE NULL
  END AS adjusted_average
FROM player_attributes pa
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT
    SUM(v) AS sum,
    MAX(v) AS max,
    MIN(v) AS min,
    COUNT(v) AS cnt
  FROM UNNEST(ARRAY[
    pa.gk_diving,
    pa.gk_handling,
    pa.gk_kicking,
    pa.gk_positioning,
    pa.gk_reflexes
  ]) AS v
) AS stats
ORDER BY pa.id;

WITH lap_stats AS (
  SELECT
    r.raceid,
    r.laps,
    COUNT(*) AS freq,
    MIN(r.resultid) AS first_occurrence
  FROM results r
  WHERE r.laps IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY r.raceid, r.laps
)
SELECT DISTINCT ON (raceid)
  raceid,
  laps AS modal_laps
FROM lap_stats
ORDER BY raceid, freq DESC, first_occurrence ASC;

WITH RECURSIVE atom_ancestors AS (
  -- Start from the target atom (use the numeric part of TR000_4 => 4)
  SELECT
    atom_id,
    parent_id,
    enabled,
    atom_id AS start_atom
  FROM connection
  WHERE atom_id = 4

  UNION ALL

  -- Walk up the parent chain
  SELECT
    c.atom_id,
    c.parent_id,
    c.enabled,
    aa.start_atom
  FROM connection c
  INNER JOIN atom_ancestors aa
    ON c.atom_id = aa.parent_id
),
-- Ancestors only (exclude the starting atom itself)
ancestors AS (
  SELECT atom_id, parent_id, enabled, start_atom
  FROM atom_ancestors
  WHERE atom_id <> start_atom
)
-- Return ancestors only if all of them are enabled (no false among ancestors)
SELECT atom_id, parent_id, enabled
FROM ancestors
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1
  FROM ancestors a
  WHERE a.enabled = false
);

WITH RECURSIVE
a(id, depth) AS (
  SELECT id, 0
  FROM lineage
  WHERE id = 1001
  UNION ALL
  SELECT l.parent_id, a.depth + 1
  FROM a
  JOIN lineage l ON l.id = a.id
  WHERE l.parent_id IS NOT NULL
),
b(id, depth) AS (
  SELECT id, 0
  FROM lineage
  WHERE id = 1201
  UNION ALL
  SELECT l.parent_id, b.depth + 1
  FROM b
  JOIN lineage l ON l.id = b.id
  WHERE l.parent_id IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT a.id AS common_ancestor_id,
       a.depth AS distance_from_a,
       b.depth AS distance_from_b
FROM a
JOIN b USING (id)
ORDER BY a.depth + b.depth
LIMIT 1;

WITH pattern_counts AS (
  SELECT
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "Examination Date")::int AS examination_year,
    "ANA Pattern",
    COUNT(*) AS c
  FROM examination
  WHERE "Examination Date" >= DATE '1993-01-01'
    AND "Examination Date" <  DATE '1997-01-01'
    AND "ANA Pattern" IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY 1, 2
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    pattern_counts.*,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (
      PARTITION BY examination_year
      ORDER BY c DESC
    ) AS rnk
  FROM pattern_counts
)
SELECT examination_year, "ANA Pattern", c
FROM ranked
WHERE rnk = 1
ORDER BY examination_year, "ANA Pattern";

WITH weights AS (
  SELECT
    customerid,
    SUM(amount)::double precision AS weight
  FROM transactions_1k
  WHERE amount > 0
  GROUP BY customerid
),
keys AS (
  -- Efraimidis-Spirakis weighted sampling without replacement
  -- key = -ln(U)/w, pick the 5 smallest keys = 5 winners in draw order
  SELECT
    customerid,
    (-LN(GREATEST(random(), 1e-16))) / weight AS key
  FROM weights
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    customerid,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY key) AS place
  FROM keys
)
SELECT
  place,
  customerid
FROM ranked
WHERE place <= 5
ORDER BY place;

SELECT e.*
FROM transactions_1k e
WHERE e.productid = 5
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM transactions_1k e2
    WHERE e2.date = e.date
      AND e2.time = e.time
      AND e2.cardid = e.cardid
      AND e2.gasstationid = e.gasstationid
      AND e2.productid = 2
  )
ORDER BY e.date ASC, e.time ASC;

SELECT
  ps.id,
  ps.name,
  COALESCE(
    array_agg(DISTINCT e.elem->>'description') FILTER (WHERE e.elem->>'description' IS NOT NULL),
    '{}'::text[]
  ) AS skills
FROM player_skills AS ps
LEFT JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(COALESCE(ps.skills::jsonb, '[]'::jsonb)) AS e(elem) ON true
GROUP BY ps.id, ps.name
ORDER BY ps.id;

select
  t1.table_name as t1_tables_missing_from_schema,
  t2.table_name
from required_tables t1
left join information_schema.tables t2
  on t1.table_name = t2.table_name
 and t2.table_schema = 'public'
where t2.table_name is null;

select
  t1.table_name as t1_table_name,
  t2.table_name as t2_extra_tables_in_schema
from required_tables t1
right join information_schema.tables t2
  on t1.table_name = t2.table_name
 and t2.table_schema = 'public'
where t1.table_name is null;

select
  case
    when rt.table_name is null then 'extra'   -- in schema, not in required_tables
    when t.table_name  is null then 'missing' -- in required_tables, not in schema
  end as kind,
  coalesce(rt.table_name, t.table_name) as table_name
from required_tables rt
full join information_schema.tables t
  on rt.table_name = t.table_name
 and t.table_schema = 'public'  -- change to your target schema if needed
where rt.table_name is null or t.table_name is null
order by kind, table_name;

SELECT
    r.raceid,
    r.name AS race_name,
    c.name AS circuit_name,
    array_agg(lt.milliseconds ORDER BY lt.lap) AS lap_times_ms
FROM laptimes AS lt
JOIN races AS r ON r.raceid = lt.raceid
JOIN drivers AS d ON d.driverid = lt.driverid
LEFT JOIN circuits AS c ON c.circuitid = r.circuitid
WHERE lt.driverid = 1
  AND d.forename = 'Lewis'
  AND d.surname = 'Hamilton'
GROUP BY r.raceid, r.name, c.name
ORDER BY r.year, r.round;

WITH RECURSIVE mgr_chain AS (
  -- start from each employee's direct manager
  SELECT
    e.id    AS employee_id,
    e.salary AS employee_salary,
    m.id    AS boss_id,
    m.salary AS boss_salary,
    m.bossid AS next_boss_id,
    1       AS hierarchical_level
  FROM staff e
  JOIN staff m ON m.id = e.bossid

  UNION ALL

  -- walk up the management chain
  SELECT
    c.employee_id,
    c.employee_salary,
    m2.id      AS boss_id,
    m2.salary  AS boss_salary,
    m2.bossid  AS next_boss_id,
    c.hierarchical_level + 1
  FROM mgr_chain c
  JOIN staff m2 ON m2.id = c.next_boss_id
),
qualified AS (
  SELECT
    employee_id,
    employee_salary,
    hierarchical_level,
    boss_id,
    boss_salary,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY employee_id ORDER BY hierarchical_level) AS rn
  FROM mgr_chain
  WHERE boss_salary >= 2 * employee_salary
)
SELECT
  e.id      AS employeeId,
  e.salary  AS employeeSalary,
  q.hierarchical_level,
  q.boss_id AS bossId,
  q.boss_salary AS bossSalary
FROM staff e
LEFT JOIN qualified q
  ON q.employee_id = e.id AND q.rn = 1
ORDER BY e.id;

WITH bounds AS (
  SELECT
    begin_date,
    end_date,
    -- start at the first full month after begin_date unless it is already the 1st
    CASE
      WHEN begin_date = date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date
        THEN date_trunc('month', begin_date)::date
      ELSE (date_trunc('month', begin_date) + interval '1 month')::date
    END AS start_month,
    -- treat end_date as exclusive: stop at the first day of its month
    date_trunc('month', end_date)::date AS end_month
  FROM race_dates
)
SELECT
  begin_date,
  end_date,
  GREATEST(
    0,
    (date_part('year', end_month)::int * 12 + date_part('month', end_month)::int)
    - (date_part('year', start_month)::int * 12 + date_part('month', start_month)::int)
  ) AS full_calendar_months
FROM bounds;

SELECT a.district_id,
       array_agg(l.loan_id) FILTER (WHERE l.status IN ('A', 'B')) AS type_a_b,
       array_agg(l.loan_id) FILTER (WHERE l.status = 'C') AS type_c
FROM account a
LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id = a.account_id
GROUP BY a.district_id
ORDER BY a.district_id;

WITH player_names AS (
  SELECT
    1 AS source,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY player_name) AS row_number,
    CONCAT(player_name, ' (', LEFT(birthday, 4), ')') AS dest
  FROM player_table
),
year_counts AS (
  SELECT
    LEFT(birthday, 4) AS birth_year,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM player_table
  GROUP BY LEFT(birthday, 4)
),
birth_year_summary AS (
  SELECT
    2 AS source,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY cnt, birth_year) AS row_number,
    CONCAT(
      'There are a total of ', cnt, ' player',
      CASE WHEN cnt > 1 THEN 's' ELSE '' END,
      ' born in ', birth_year, '.'
    ) AS dest
  FROM year_counts
)
SELECT dest
FROM (
  SELECT source, row_number, dest FROM player_names
  UNION ALL
  SELECT source, row_number, dest FROM birth_year_summary
) AS combined_results
ORDER BY source, row_number;

WITH max_dates AS (
  SELECT id, MAX(date) AS max_date
  FROM laboratory
  GROUP BY id
),
max_pairs AS (
  -- all (got, gpt) pairs observed on the max date per patient
  SELECT l.id, l.got, l.gpt
  FROM laboratory l
  JOIN max_dates md
    ON l.id = md.id
   AND l.date = md.max_date
  GROUP BY l.id, l.got, l.gpt
),
earliest_match AS (
  -- for each patient, pick the earliest row whose (got, gpt) matches
  -- any (got, gpt) seen on that patient's max date
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (l.id)
         l.id, l.date, l.got, l.gpt
  FROM laboratory l
  JOIN max_pairs mp
    ON l.id = mp.id
   AND l.got IS NOT DISTINCT FROM mp.got
   AND l.gpt IS NOT DISTINCT FROM mp.gpt
  ORDER BY l.id, l.date ASC
)
SELECT id, date, got, gpt
FROM earliest_match
ORDER BY id, date;

WITH target_type AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT c.type AS card_type
  FROM card c
  WHERE c.disp_id = 41
)
UPDATE card c
SET disp_id = CASE
  WHEN c.disp_id = 41 THEN 1
  WHEN c.disp_id < 41 THEN c.disp_id + 1
  ELSE c.disp_id
END
FROM target_type t
WHERE c.type = t.card_type
  AND c.disp_id <= 41;

WITH runs AS (
  SELECT
    sa.superhero_id,
    sa.activity_code,
    sa.ts,
    -- Key that stays constant for contiguous 5-minute steps within the same hero/activity
    sa.ts - (ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY sa.superhero_id, sa.activity_code ORDER BY sa.ts)) * INTERVAL '5 minutes' AS grp_key
  FROM superhero_activities sa
),
agg AS (
  SELECT
    superhero_id,
    activity_code,
    grp_key,
    MIN(ts) AS start_ts,
    MAX(ts) AS last_ts,
    COUNT(*) AS steps
  FROM runs
  GROUP BY superhero_id, activity_code, grp_key
)
SELECT
  superhero_id,
  activity_code,
  start_ts,
  last_ts + INTERVAL '5 minutes' AS end_ts,
  steps * 5 AS total_minutes
FROM agg
ORDER BY superhero_id, activity_code, start_ts;

WITH
qr1(item, subgroup) AS (
  SELECT id, subgroup1
  FROM superhero_group
  WHERE subgroup1 IS NOT NULL
),
qr2(item, subgroup) AS (
  SELECT id, subgroup2
  FROM superhero_group
  WHERE subgroup2 IS NOT NULL
),
items AS (
  SELECT item FROM qr1
  UNION
  SELECT item FROM qr2
),
-- tag each assignment so same numeric values across q1/q2 don't collide
assignments AS (
  SELECT item, ('q1:' || subgroup::text) AS grp_key FROM qr1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT item, ('q2:' || subgroup::text) AS grp_key FROM qr2
),
-- build undirected edges between items that share a subgroup in either query
edges AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT a1.item AS src, a2.item AS dst
  FROM assignments a1
  JOIN assignments a2
    ON a1.grp_key = a2.grp_key
   AND a1.item <> a2.item
),
-- transitive closure to get all items reachable from each item
RECURSIVE reach(start_item, reached) AS (
  SELECT i.item, i.item
  FROM items i
  UNION
  SELECT r.start_item, e.dst
  FROM reach r
  JOIN edges e
    ON e.src = r.reached
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM reach r2
    WHERE r2.start_item = r.start_item
      AND r2.reached = e.dst
  )
),
-- pick a stable component id per connected component (min item id)
component AS (
  SELECT start_item AS item, MIN(reached) AS comp_id
  FROM reach
  GROUP BY start_item
),
combined AS (
  SELECT i.item,
         q1.subgroup AS subgroup1,
         q2.subgroup AS subgroup2
  FROM items i
  LEFT JOIN qr1 q1 ON q1.item = i.item
  LEFT JOIN qr2 q2 ON q2.item = i.item
)
SELECT
  c.item,
  c.subgroup1,
  c.subgroup2,
  DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY comp.comp_id) AS group_id
FROM combined c
JOIN component comp
  ON comp.item = c.item
ORDER BY group_id, c.item;

WITH earnings_base AS (
  SELECT
    driverid,
    points,
    points * 100 AS earnings
  FROM driverstandings
)
SELECT
  driverid,
  points,
  earnings,
  CASE
    WHEN earnings > 5000 THEN earnings * 0.2
    WHEN earnings > 3000 THEN earnings * 0.15
    ELSE NULL
  END AS bonus
FROM earnings_base;

WITH days AS (
  SELECT gs::date AS the_day
  FROM generate_series(timestamp '2020-01-01', timestamp '2020-03-31', interval '1 day') AS gs
),
event_counts AS (
  SELECT
    (e.event_date::timestamp)::date AS the_day,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE e.status = 'Open') AS open_events,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE e.status = 'Closed') AS closed_events
  FROM "event" e
  WHERE (e.event_date::timestamp)::date BETWEEN date '2020-01-01' AND date '2020-03-31'
  GROUP BY (e.event_date::timestamp)::date
),
avg_remaining AS (
  SELECT
    (e.event_date::timestamp)::date AS the_day,
    AVG(b.remaining) AS avg_remaining
  FROM "event" e
  JOIN budget b ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
  WHERE (e.event_date::timestamp)::date BETWEEN date '2020-01-01' AND date '2020-03-31'
  GROUP BY (e.event_date::timestamp)::date
)
SELECT
  d.the_day AS "Date",
  ar.avg_remaining AS "Avg Remaining Budget",
  COALESCE(ec.open_events, 0) AS "Open Events",
  COALESCE(ec.closed_events, 0) AS "Closed Events"
FROM days d
LEFT JOIN avg_remaining ar ON ar.the_day = d.the_day
LEFT JOIN event_counts ec ON ec.the_day = d.the_day
ORDER BY d.the_day;

SELECT
  pat.id,
  MIN(lab.date) AS firstActivity
FROM patient AS pat
JOIN laboratory AS lab
  ON lab.id = pat.id
WHERE
  pat.sex = 'F'
  AND lab.date > pat."First Date"
  AND lab.date <= pat.description
GROUP BY
  pat.id
ORDER BY
  pat.id;

with adjacency as (
  select atom_id as id, atom_id2 as neighbor from connected
  union
  select atom_id2 as id, atom_id as neighbor from connected
),
degree as (
  select id, count(*) as num
  from adjacency
  group by id
),
max_degree as (
  select max(num) as max_num from degree
)
select d.id, d.num
from degree d
join max_degree m on d.num = m.max_num
order by d.id;

-- 1) One row per token (explodes matches into separate rows)
--    Returns the token including braces, e.g. {{T}}, {{1}}, {{W}}, etc.
SELECT
  c.id,
  c."text" AS card_text,
  '{{' || m[1] || '}}' AS token
FROM cards AS c
CROSS JOIN LATERAL regexp_matches(c."text", '\{\{([^}]+)\}\}', 'g') AS m;

-- 2) Tokens aggregated per row as an array + count of tokens
SELECT
  c.id,
  c."text" AS card_text,
  COALESCE(array_agg('{{' || m[1] || '}}') FILTER (WHERE m[1] IS NOT NULL), '{}') AS bracketed_tokens,
  COUNT(m[1]) AS token_count
FROM cards AS c
LEFT JOIN LATERAL regexp_matches(c."text", '\{\{([^}]+)\}\}', 'g') AS m ON true
GROUP BY c.id, c."text";

-- 3) Optional: tokens aggregated as a comma-separated string
SELECT
  c.id,
  c."text" AS card_text,
  COALESCE(string_agg('{{' || m[1] || '}}', ', ') FILTER (WHERE m[1] IS NOT NULL), '') AS token_list,
  COUNT(m[1]) AS token_count
FROM cards AS c
LEFT JOIN LATERAL regexp_matches(c."text", '\{\{([^}]+)\}\}', 'g') AS m ON true
GROUP BY c.id, c."text";

SELECT
  r.raceid AS race_id,
  c.constructor_ids,
  s.stops
FROM races r
LEFT JOIN (
  -- One constructor ID per race (no duplicates), ordered by the earliest result row for that constructor in the race
  SELECT
    raceid,
    string_agg(constructorid::text, ',' ORDER BY first_result_id) AS constructor_ids
  FROM (
    SELECT raceid, constructorid, MIN(resultid) AS first_result_id
    FROM results
    GROUP BY raceid, constructorid
  ) rc
  GROUP BY raceid
) c ON c.raceid = r.raceid
LEFT JOIN (
  -- Unique stop numbers per race (no duplicates), ordered numerically
  SELECT
    raceid,
    string_agg(stop::text, ', ' ORDER BY stop) AS stops
  FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT raceid, stop
    FROM pitstops
  ) ps
  GROUP BY raceid
) s ON s.raceid = r.raceid
ORDER BY r.raceid;

SELECT resultid
FROM (
  SELECT resultid
  FROM results
  WHERE driverid = 3
  ORDER BY resultid DESC
  LIMIT 10

  UNION ALL

  SELECT resultid
  FROM results
  WHERE driverid = 4
  ORDER BY resultid DESC
  LIMIT 10
) AS t
ORDER BY resultid DESC
LIMIT 10;

WITH drivers_by_pos AS (
  SELECT position, driverid
  FROM results
  WHERE position IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY position, driverid
)
SELECT
  position,
  array_remove(array_agg(driverid ORDER BY driverid), max(driverid)) AS driverids
FROM drivers_by_pos
GROUP BY position
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY position;

WITH parsed AS (
  SELECT
    version,
    regexp_match(version, '^(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)(?:-([0-9]+))?') AS parts
  FROM cars
)
SELECT version
FROM parsed
ORDER BY
  COALESCE((parts[1])::int, 0) DESC,  -- major
  COALESCE((parts[2])::int, 0) DESC,  -- minor
  COALESCE((parts[3])::int, 0) DESC,  -- patch
  COALESCE((parts[4])::int, 0) DESC,  -- revision after dash (e.g., -1)
  version DESC;

SELECT
  ml.match_id,
  ml.current_lineup,
  ml.next_lineup,
  (
    SELECT COUNT(*)
    FROM (
      SELECT DISTINCT c.player_id
      FROM unnest(ml.current_lineup) AS c(player_id)
      WHERE c.player_id IS NOT NULL
      INTERSECT
      SELECT DISTINCT n.player_id
      FROM unnest(ml.next_lineup) AS n(player_id)
      WHERE n.player_id IS NOT NULL
    ) AS common_players
  ) AS common_player_count
FROM match_lineups AS ml;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION words_get_chat(in_gid integer, in_social integer, in_sid text)
RETURNS TABLE (out_mine integer, out_msg text)
LANGUAGE sql
AS $func$
  SELECT
    CASE WHEN c.uid = s.uid THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS out_mine,
    c.msg AS out_msg
  FROM words_chat AS c
  JOIN words_games AS g USING (gid)
  JOIN words_social AS s
    ON s.uid IN (g.player1, g.player2)
  JOIN words_users AS u
    ON u.uid = c.uid
  WHERE
    c.gid = in_gid
    AND s.social = in_social
    AND s.sid = in_sid
    -- Show messages if the sender is not muted, or if the viewer is the sender
    AND (u.muted = false OR c.uid = s.uid)
  ORDER BY c.created ASC;
$func$;

-- Get all teams that have played in a given league (no recursion needed; there is no league hierarchy)
SELECT DISTINCT
  t.team_api_id,
  t.team_long_name,
  t.team_short_name
FROM "match" m
JOIN team t
  ON t.team_api_id IN (m.home_team_api_id, m.away_team_api_id)
WHERE m.league_id = $1  -- replace $1 with the target league_id
ORDER BY t.team_long_name;

WITH tx AS (
  SELECT
    t.transactionid,
    t.date,
    t.time,
    t.customerid,
    t.cardid,
    t.gasstationid,
    t.productid,
    t.amount,
    t.price,
    -- Build a proper timestamp for correct chronological ordering
    t.date::timestamp + COALESCE(t.time, '00:00:00')::time AS ts
  FROM transactions_1k t
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    tx.*,
    -- Last transaction per (customer, gasstation)
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid
      ORDER BY ts DESC, transactionid DESC
    ) AS rn_last,
    -- First occurrence of amount < 10 per (customer, gasstation)
    CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid
        ORDER BY ts ASC, transactionid ASC
      )
    END AS rn_first_issue,
    -- Does this (customer, gasstation) have any issue at all?
    SUM(CASE WHEN amount < 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY customerid, gasstationid) AS issue_cnt
  FROM tx
)
SELECT
  customerid,
  transactionid,
  gasstationid,
  amount,
  date,
  time,
  price
FROM ranked
WHERE
  -- If there was any issue at this station, keep the first such occurrence
  (issue_cnt > 0 AND rn_first_issue = 1)
  -- Otherwise, keep the last transaction at this station
  OR (issue_cnt = 0 AND rn_last = 1)
ORDER BY customerid, gasstationid, date, time;

-- Recalculate eff_to only for the affected team, using the next record's date per team
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_team_attributes_eff_to()
RETURNS TRIGGER
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $$
BEGIN
  -- If team_api_id is null, nothing to do
  IF NEW.team_api_id IS NULL THEN
    RETURN NULL;
  END IF;

  WITH ordered AS (
    SELECT
      id,
      LEAD((date::timestamp)::date) OVER (
        PARTITION BY team_api_id
        ORDER BY (date::timestamp)::date, id
      ) AS next_date
    FROM team_attributes
    WHERE team_api_id = NEW.team_api_id
  )
  UPDATE team_attributes t
  SET eff_to = COALESCE((o.next_date - INTERVAL '1 day')::date, DATE '5999-12-31')
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE t.id = o.id;

  RETURN NULL; -- AFTER trigger
END;
$$;

-- Recreate the trigger to call the corrected function
DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS after_insert_team_attributes ON team_attributes;

CREATE TRIGGER after_insert_team_attributes
AFTER INSERT ON team_attributes
FOR EACH ROW
EXECUTE PROCEDURE update_team_attributes_eff_to();

alloc AS (

)
SELECT
  p.start_ts
    + floor(extract(epoch FROM (e."TIMESTAMP" - p.start_ts))
            / extract(epoch FROM p.step)) * p.step AS interval_start,
  COUNT(*) AS cnt,
  AVG(e."CL") AS cl,
  AVG(e."C") AS c

  ),
  params(n, k_prev, k_next) AS (
    VALUES (2, 2, 2)  -- n = 2, k = 2, k' = 2
  ),
  ordered AS (
    SELECT
      row_number() OVER (ORDER BY hero_id) AS rn,
      attribute_value
    FROM hero_attribute
  ),
  blocks AS (
    SELECT generate_series(
             0,
             (SELECT (count(*) - 1) / n FROM ordered, params)
           ) AS block_id
  )

  SUM(o.attribute_value) AS sum
FROM blocks b
CROSS JOIN params p
JOIN ordered o
  ON o.rn BETWEEN b.block_id * p.n + 1 - p.k_prev

-- Drop and recreate a simple, polymorphic forward-fill aggregate

DROP AGGREGATE IF EXISTS public.ffill(anyelement);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.ffill_state(anyelement, anyelement);

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.ffill_state(state anyelement, val anyelement)
RETURNS anyelement
LANGUAGE plpgsql
IMMUTABLE
AS $$
BEGIN
  IF val IS NOT NULL THEN
    RETURN val;           -- take the newest non-null
  ELSE
    RETURN state;         -- otherwise keep the last seen value
  END IF;
END
$$;

CREATE AGGREGATE public.ffill(anyelement)
(
  SFUNC = public.ffill_state,
  STYPE = anyelement
);

)

WHERE e.start_time >= b.min_start

WITH RECURSIVE
params AS (
  SELECT 20::integer AS threshold  -- set your threshold here
),
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    amount,
    row_number() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
  FROM transfer
),
cte AS (
  -- anchor row (first by id)
  SELECT
    o.rn,
    o.id,
    o.amount,
    o.amount AS cum_sum,
    1 AS bucket
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE o.rn = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- recursive step: accumulate until threshold, then reset and bump bucket
  SELECT
    o.rn,
    o.id,
    o.amount,
    CASE WHEN c.cum_sum + o.amount > p.threshold
         THEN o.amount
         ELSE c.cum_sum + o.amount
    END AS cum_sum,
    CASE WHEN c.cum_sum + o.amount > p.threshold
         THEN c.bucket + 1
         ELSE c.bucket
    END AS bucket
  FROM cte c
  JOIN ordered o
    ON o.rn = c.rn + 1
  CROSS JOIN params p
)
SELECT id, amount, cum_sum, bucket
FROM cte
ORDER BY id;

WITH vars AS (
  SELECT DATE '2022-01-27' AS today
),
dates AS (
  SELECT
    (today - 1) AS yday,
    (today - 8) AS day_8_ago,
    date_trunc('month', today - 1)::date AS curr_month_start,
    (date_trunc('month', today - 1) - interval '1 month')::date AS prev_month_start,
    EXTRACT(day FROM (today - 1))::int AS yday_dom
  FROM vars
),
agg AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(CASE WHEN t."timestamp" = d.yday THEN t.amount END) AS yesterday_revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t."timestamp" = d.day_8_ago THEN t.amount END) AS eight_days_ago_revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t."timestamp" >= d.curr_month_start AND t."timestamp" <= d.yday THEN t.amount END) AS mtd,
    SUM(CASE WHEN t."timestamp" >= d.prev_month_start
              AND t."timestamp" < (d.prev_month_start + interval '1 month')
              AND EXTRACT(day FROM t."timestamp") <= d.yday_dom
             THEN t.amount END) AS prev_mtd
  FROM transactions t
  CROSS JOIN dates d
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(yesterday_revenue, 0) AS yesterday_revenue,
  ROUND((yesterday_revenue - eight_days_ago_revenue) / NULLIF(eight_days_ago_revenue, 0), 2) AS pct_change_week_ago,
  COALESCE(mtd, 0) AS mtd,
  ROUND((mtd - prev_mtd) / NULLIF(prev_mtd, 0), 2) AS pct_change_month_prior
FROM agg;

WITH RECURSIVE
ordered AS (
  SELECT
    id,
    trx::numeric AS trx,
    event::numeric AS event,
    row_number() OVER (ORDER BY id) AS rn
  FROM sales_transactions
),
rec AS (
  -- Anchor row (first id)
  SELECT
    o.id,
    o.rn,
    o.trx,
    o.event,
    o.trx AS cum_trx,                 -- cumulative trx up to current row
    CAST(0 AS numeric) AS sum_prev_results, -- sum of results up to previous row
    o.event * (o.trx + 0) AS result   -- event * (cum_trx + sum_prev_results)
  FROM ordered o
  WHERE o.rn = 1

  UNION ALL

  -- Recursive step
  SELECT
    o.id,
    o.rn,
    o.trx,
    o.event,
    r.cum_trx + o.trx AS cum_trx,
    r.sum_prev_results + r.result AS sum_prev_results,
    o.event * ((r.cum_trx + o.trx) + (r.sum_prev_results + r.result)) AS result
  FROM rec r
  JOIN ordered o
    ON o.rn = r.rn + 1
)
SELECT
  id,
  round(result, 6)::numeric(38, 6) AS result
FROM rec
ORDER BY id;

  SELECT
    entity_id,
    updated_at,
    NULLIF(change->>'height', 'null')::bigint AS height_val,
    NULLIF(change->>'weight', 'null')::bigint AS weight_val
  FROM changes
),
with_rn AS (
  SELECT
    v.*,
    row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY updated_at) AS rn
  FROM vals v
),
ff AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    max(CASE WHEN height_val IS NOT NULL THEN rn END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY rn
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS last_h_rn,
    max(CASE WHEN weight_val IS NOT NULL THEN rn END)
      OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY rn
            ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS last_w_rn
  FROM with_rn
)
SELECT
  entity_id,
  updated_at,
  first_value(height_val) OVER (
    PARTITION BY entity_id, last_h_rn
    ORDER BY rn
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
  ) AS height,
  first_value(weight_val) OVER (
    PARTITION BY entity_id, last_w_rn
    ORDER BY rn
    ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
  ) AS weight
FROM ff
ORDER BY entity_id, updated_at;

WITH RECURSIVE leaf_to_root AS (
    -- start from leaves (nodes with no children); they already have a color
    SELECT s.id, s.parent_id, s.color AS leaf_color
    FROM superhero2 s
    WHERE s.color IS NOT NULL
      AND NOT EXISTS (
          SELECT 1
          FROM superhero2 ch
          WHERE ch.parent_id = s.id
      )
  UNION ALL
    -- move one step up carrying each leaf's color to all its ancestors
    SELECT p.id, p.parent_id, l.leaf_color
    FROM leaf_to_root l
    JOIN superhero2 p
      ON p.id = l.parent_id
),
colored AS (
    -- for each node, if all descendant leaves have the same color, use it; otherwise gray
    SELECT id,
           CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT leaf_color) = 1
                THEN MIN(leaf_color)
                ELSE 'gray'
           END AS color
    FROM leaf_to_root
    GROUP BY id
)
UPDATE superhero2 s
SET color = c.color
FROM colored c
WHERE s.id = c.id;

-- Distinct values for a single key ('dis') per customer
SELECT cp.customerid,
       to_jsonb(array_agg(DISTINCT e.elem ORDER BY e.elem)) AS dis_values
FROM card_preference AS cp
CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements_text(
  CASE
    WHEN jsonb_typeof(cp.preference->'dis') = 'array' THEN cp.preference->'dis'
    WHEN cp.preference ? 'dis' THEN jsonb_build_array(cp.preference->'dis')
    ELSE '[]'::jsonb
  END
) AS e(elem)
WHERE cp.customerid = '123.abc'
GROUP BY cp.customerid;


-- Distinct values for ALL keys per customer (builds a JSON object: key -> distinct values)
SELECT customerid,
       jsonb_object_agg(key, vals) AS aggregated_preferences
FROM (
  SELECT customerid, key, jsonb_agg(DISTINCT el) AS vals
  FROM (
    SELECT cp.customerid,
           je.key,
           a.el
    FROM card_preference AS cp
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_each(cp.preference) AS je(key, val)
    CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(
      CASE
        WHEN jsonb_typeof(je.val) = 'array' THEN je.val
        ELSE jsonb_build_array(je.val)
      END
    ) AS a(el)
    WHERE cp.customerid = '123.abc'  -- remove this line to compute for all customers
  ) AS s
  GROUP BY customerid, key
) AS g
GROUP BY customerid;

-- Aggregate report stats to the granularity of (id, utm_campaign, utm_source, utm_medium)
-- using utm as the driving table. NULL (or empty string) in utm acts as a wildcard,
-- so content/term (and any other NULL utm dimension) are summed across.

WITH utm_norm AS (
  SELECT
    row_id,
    NULLIF(utm_campaign, '') AS utm_campaign,
    NULLIF(utm_source,   '') AS utm_source,
    NULLIF(utm_medium,   '') AS utm_medium,
    NULLIF(utm_content,  '') AS utm_content,
    NULLIF(utm_term,     '') AS utm_term
  FROM utm
)
SELECT
  u.row_id AS id,
  u.utm_campaign,
  u.utm_source,
  u.utm_medium,
  COALESCE(s.sessions,  0) AS sessions,
  COALESCE(s.pageviews, 0) AS pageviews,
  COALESCE(s.bounces,   0) AS bounces
FROM utm_norm u
LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
  SELECT
    SUM(r.sessions)  AS sessions,
    SUM(r.pageviews) AS pageviews,
    SUM(r.bounces)   AS bounces
  FROM report r
  WHERE r.id = u.row_id
    -- treat NULL (or empty) values in UTM as "wildcards"
    AND (u.utm_campaign IS NULL OR r.utm_campaign = u.utm_campaign)
    AND (u.utm_source   IS NULL OR r.utm_source   = u.utm_source)
    AND (u.utm_medium   IS NULL OR r.utm_medium   = u.utm_medium)
    AND (u.utm_content  IS NULL OR r.utm_content  = u.utm_content)
    AND (u.utm_term     IS NULL OR r.utm_term     = u.utm_term)
    -- exclude explicit 'NA' markers in report UTM fields
    AND r.utm_campaign IS DISTINCT FROM 'NA'
    AND r.utm_source   IS DISTINCT FROM 'NA'
    AND r.utm_medium   IS DISTINCT FROM 'NA'
    AND r.utm_content  IS DISTINCT FROM 'NA'
    AND r.utm_term     IS DISTINCT FROM 'NA'
) s ON TRUE;

-- For each parent card_type, return its direct children grouped together.
-- Includes both an array of child UUIDs and a JSON array of {uuid, card_name}.
-- Restrict to roots (parent_uuid IS NULL). Remove that WHERE to list all parents.
SELECT
  p.uuid AS parent_uuid,
  p.card_name AS parent_name,
  COALESCE(
    array_agg(c.uuid) FILTER (WHERE c.uuid IS NOT NULL),
    ARRAY[]::uuid[]
  ) AS child_uuids,
  COALESCE(
    jsonb_agg(
      jsonb_build_object('uuid', c.uuid, 'card_name', c.card_name)
      ORDER BY c.card_name
    ) FILTER (WHERE c.uuid IS NOT NULL),
    '[]'::jsonb
  ) AS children
FROM card_type AS p
LEFT JOIN card_type AS c
  ON c.parent_uuid = p.uuid
WHERE p.parent_uuid IS NULL
GROUP BY p.uuid, p.card_name
ORDER BY p.card_name;

WITH latest_loan_per_acct AS (
  SELECT
    a.account_id,
    a.frequency,
    a.date AS account_date,
    l.loan_id,
    l.date AS loan_date,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY a.account_id
      ORDER BY l.date DESC NULLS LAST, l.loan_id DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM account a
  LEFT JOIN loan l ON l.account_id = a.account_id
  WHERE a.district_id = 18
),
top6 AS (
  SELECT lpa.account_id
  FROM latest_loan_per_acct lpa
  WHERE lpa.rn = 1
    AND lpa.loan_date::timestamp >= (NOW() - INTERVAL '48 hours')
  ORDER BY lpa.loan_date DESC NULLS LAST
  LIMIT 6
)
SELECT
  lpa.account_id,
  lpa.frequency,
  lpa.loan_id,
  lpa.loan_date,
  CASE WHEN t.account_id IS NOT NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS priority
FROM latest_loan_per_acct lpa
LEFT JOIN top6 t ON t.account_id = lpa.account_id
WHERE lpa.rn = 1
ORDER BY
  CASE WHEN t.account_id IS NOT NULL THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,                     -- priority: recent top-6 first
  CASE WHEN t.account_id IS NOT NULL THEN lpa.loan_date END DESC NULLS LAST, -- order top-6 by most recent loan_date
  CASE WHEN t.account_id IS NULL THEN lpa.account_date END DESC NULLS LAST;  -- order the rest by account.date

SELECT MIN(price) AS min_price
FROM (
  -- prices within the last 30 days, excluding the new price itself
  SELECT price
  FROM card_prices
  WHERE uuid = '5f8287b1-5bb6-5f4c-ad17-316a40d5bb0c'
    AND price > 0
    AND "timestamp" >= '2022-07-12T15:30:00-00:00'::timestamptz
    AND "timestamp" <  '2022-08-11T15:30:00-00:00'::timestamptz

  UNION ALL

  -- the last price strictly before the 30-day window
  SELECT price
  FROM card_prices
  WHERE uuid = '5f8287b1-5bb6-5f4c-ad17-316a40d5bb0c'
    AND price > 0
    AND "timestamp" = (
      SELECT MAX("timestamp")
      FROM card_prices
      WHERE uuid = '5f8287b1-5bb6-5f4c-ad17-316a40d5bb0c'
        AND "timestamp" < '2022-07-12T15:30:00-00:00'::timestamptz
    )
) AS prices;

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Retail price per item (some items have multiple rows; take a single price)
prices AS (
  SELECT item_id, MAX(item_price) AS retail_price
  FROM store
  GROUP BY item_id
),
-- Cost from resources only (partial craft price)
resource_cost AS (
  SELECT item_id,
         SUM(COALESCE(resource_price, 0) * COALESCE(quantity, 1)) AS partial_craft_price
  FROM store
  WHERE resource_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY item_id
),
-- Components (other items) needed to craft each item
components AS (
  SELECT item_id,
         uses_item_id AS component_id,
         SUM(COALESCE(quantity, 1)) AS qty
  FROM store
  WHERE uses_item_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY item_id, uses_item_id
),
-- All items we care about with their retail and partial craft prices
all_items AS (
  SELECT p.item_id,
         p.retail_price,
         COALESCE(rc.partial_craft_price, 0) AS partial_craft_price
  FROM prices p
  LEFT JOIN resource_cost rc USING (item_id)
),
-- Iteratively resolve the minimal cost to obtain each item:
-- min_cost = LEAST(retail_price, full_craft_price)
-- full_craft_price = resource cost + sum(qty * min_cost(child))
resolved AS (
  -- Seed: items without component dependencies (only resources)
  SELECT ai.item_id,
         ai.retail_price,
         ai.partial_craft_price AS full_craft_price,
         LEAST(ai.retail_price, ai.partial_craft_price) AS min_cost
  FROM all_items ai
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM components c WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id)

  UNION ALL

  -- Step: items whose components are all already resolved
  SELECT ai.item_id,
         ai.retail_price,
         ai.partial_craft_price
         + (
             SELECT SUM(c.qty * r.min_cost)
             FROM components c
             JOIN resolved r ON r.item_id = c.component_id
             WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id
           ) AS full_craft_price,
         LEAST(
           ai.retail_price,
           ai.partial_craft_price
           + (
               SELECT SUM(c.qty * r.min_cost)
               FROM components c
               JOIN resolved r ON r.item_id = c.component_id
               WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id
             )
         ) AS min_cost
  FROM all_items ai
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (
          SELECT 1
          FROM components c
          WHERE c.item_id = ai.item_id
            AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM resolved r WHERE r.item_id = c.component_id)
        )
    AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM resolved r0 WHERE r0.item_id = ai.item_id)
)
SELECT ai.item_id,
       ai.retail_price AS retail_price,
       ai.partial_craft_price AS partial_craft_price,
       r.full_craft_price AS full_craft_price
FROM all_items ai
LEFT JOIN resolved r ON r.item_id = ai.item_id
ORDER BY ai.item_id;

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION f_gap_fill_update(tbl regclass, id text, row_num text)
RETURNS void
LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $func$
DECLARE
    v_schema text;
    v_table  text;
    fqtn     text;
    col      text;
BEGIN
    -- Resolve schema and table names from regclass
    SELECT n.nspname, c.relname
      INTO v_schema, v_table
      FROM pg_class c
      JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace
     WHERE c.oid = tbl;

    fqtn := format('%I.%I', v_schema, v_table);

    -- Loop over all nullable columns except the partition and order columns
    FOR col IN
        SELECT c.column_name
          FROM information_schema.columns c
         WHERE c.table_schema = v_schema
           AND c.table_name  = v_table
           AND c.is_nullable = 'YES'
           AND c.column_name <> id
           AND c.column_name <> row_num
    LOOP
        -- Forward-fill each nullable column within partition id ordered by row_num
        EXECUTE format($sql$
            WITH src AS (
                SELECT
                    ctid,
                    %1$I AS val,
                    %2$I AS id_col,
                    sum(CASE WHEN %1$I IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
                        OVER (PARTITION BY %2$I ORDER BY %3$I, ctid) AS grp
                FROM %4$s
            ),
            fill AS (
                SELECT
                    ctid,
                    max(val) OVER (PARTITION BY id_col, grp) AS filled
                FROM src
            )
            UPDATE %4$s t
               SET %1$I = f.filled
              FROM fill f
             WHERE t.ctid = f.ctid
               AND t.%1$I IS NULL
               AND f.filled IS NOT NULL;
        $sql$, col, id, row_num, fqtn);
    END LOOP;
END
$func$;

-- 1) Ensure a unique key exists on (id, stage) so it can be referenced by FKs
ALTER TABLE "match"
ADD CONSTRAINT match_id_stage_unique UNIQUE (id, stage);

-- 2) Replace the problematic FK constraints with cascading composite FKs
ALTER TABLE "dependency"
DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS dependency_match1_stage1_fkey,
DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS dependency_match2_stage2_fkey;

ALTER TABLE "dependency"
ADD CONSTRAINT dependency_match1_stage1_fkey
  FOREIGN KEY (match1, stage1)
  REFERENCES "match" (id, stage)
  ON UPDATE CASCADE
  ON DELETE CASCADE,
ADD CONSTRAINT dependency_match2_stage2_fkey
  FOREIGN KEY (match2, stage2)
  REFERENCES "match" (id, stage)
  ON UPDATE CASCADE
  ON DELETE CASCADE;

-- 3) Now you can safely bump the stage and dependencies will update automatically
UPDATE "match"
SET stage = stage + 1
WHERE id = 'match1';

WITH card_activity AS (
  SELECT
    to_date(fe.start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD') AS start_date,
    to_date(fe.end_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD')   AS end_date,
    dp.set_name,
    dp.set_code
  FROM fact_collection fe
  JOIN dim_set dp ON fe.set_key = dp.set_key
),
bounds AS (
  SELECT
    date_trunc('month', MIN(to_date(start_date_key::text, 'YYYYMMDD')))::date AS min_month,
    date_trunc('month', COALESCE(
      MAX(to_date(COALESCE(end_date_key, start_date_key)::text, 'YYYYMMDD')),
      CURRENT_DATE
    ))::date AS max_month
  FROM fact_collection
),
months AS (
  SELECT gs::date AS month_start,
         (gs + interval '1 month')::date AS next_month_start
  FROM bounds b,
       generate_series(b.min_month, b.max_month, interval '1 month') AS gs
)
SELECT
  m.month_start AS month,
  sa.set_name,
  COUNT(sa.set_name) AS existing_cards
FROM months m
LEFT JOIN card_activity sa
  ON sa.start_date < m.next_month_start            -- started before month ends
 AND (sa.end_date IS NULL OR sa.end_date >= m.month_start)  -- not ended before month starts
GROUP BY m.month_start, sa.set_name
HAVING COUNT(sa.set_name) > 0
ORDER BY m.month_start, sa.set_name;

-- Solution A (recommended if you can change the schema):
-- Make the primary key DEFERRABLE so uniqueness is checked at commit, not per-row update.
-- One-time schema change:
ALTER TABLE card_versions
  DROP CONSTRAINT card_versions_pkey,
  ADD CONSTRAINT card_versions_pkey
    PRIMARY KEY (seqnr, defn_id, attr_id)
    DEFERRABLE INITIALLY IMMEDIATE;

-- Then perform the update with the constraint deferred:
BEGIN;
SET CONSTRAINTS card_versions_pkey DEFERRED;

UPDATE card_versions
SET seqnr = seqnr + 1
WHERE defn_id = 100
  AND attr_id = 100
  AND seqnr >= 1;

COMMIT;


-- Solution B (no schema change): two-step "park then shift" update to avoid collisions.
-- This moves the target rows out of the way, then into their final values.
BEGIN;

WITH oldmax AS (
  SELECT MAX(seqnr)::int AS m
  FROM card_versions
  WHERE defn_id = 100 AND attr_id = 100
),
park AS (
  UPDATE card_versions cv
  SET seqnr = cv.seqnr + om.m
  FROM oldmax om
  WHERE cv.defn_id = 100
    AND cv.attr_id = 100
    AND cv.seqnr >= 1
  RETURNING cv.seqnr
)
UPDATE card_versions cv
SET seqnr = cv.seqnr - (om.m - 1)
FROM oldmax om
WHERE cv.defn_id = 100
  AND cv.attr_id = 100
  AND cv.seqnr > om.m;

COMMIT;

WITH RECURSIVE
-- Build undirected edges between users that share ref1 or share ref2
edges AS (
  SELECT u1.id AS src, u2.id AS dst
  FROM users u1
  JOIN users u2
    ON u1.ref1 IS NOT NULL AND u2.ref1 IS NOT NULL AND u1.ref1 = u2.ref1
  UNION
  SELECT u1.id AS src, u2.id AS dst
  FROM users u1
  JOIN users u2
    ON u1.ref2 IS NOT NULL AND u2.ref2 IS NOT NULL AND u1.ref2 = u2.ref2
),
-- Transitive closure over the user graph
reach AS (
  SELECT u.id AS root, u.id AS member
  FROM users u
  UNION
  SELECT r.root, e.dst
  FROM reach r
  JOIN edges e ON e.src = r.member
),
-- Pick a canonical group id per connected component (the smallest user.id)
comp AS (
  SELECT member, MIN(root) AS group_id
  FROM reach
  GROUP BY member
),
-- Members per group with their refs
group_members AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, u.id, u.username, u.ref1, u.ref2
  FROM comp c
  JOIN users u ON u.id = c.member
),
-- Aggregate user info per group
group_users AS (
  SELECT group_id::text AS group_key,
         ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT id ORDER BY id) AS ids,
         ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT username ORDER BY username) AS usernames,
         ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ref1 ORDER BY ref1) AS refs1,
         ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ref2 ORDER BY ref2) AS refs2
  FROM group_members
  GROUP BY group_id
),
-- Link assets to groups via any ref1/ref2 in the group
assets_in_groups AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT gm.group_id::text AS group_key, a.id AS asset_id
  FROM group_members gm
  JOIN assets a ON a.owner = gm.ref1 OR a.owner = gm.ref2
),
-- Aggregate assets per existing user group
aggregated_groups AS (
  SELECT gu.group_key,
         gu.ids, gu.usernames, gu.refs1, gu.refs2,
         COUNT(DISTINCT ag.asset_id) AS asset_count,
         ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT ag.asset_id ORDER BY ag.asset_id) AS asset_ids,
         MIN(ag.asset_id) AS min_asset_id
  FROM group_users gu
  JOIN assets_in_groups ag USING (group_key)
  GROUP BY gu.group_key, gu.ids, gu.usernames, gu.refs1, gu.refs2
),
-- Assets whose owner isn't present in users(ref1/ref2)
orphan_assets AS (
  SELECT a.owner, a.id AS asset_id
  FROM assets a
  LEFT JOIN users u ON a.owner = u.ref1 OR a.owner = u.ref2
  WHERE u.id IS NULL AND a.owner IS NOT NULL
),
-- For orphans: propagate owner into refs2, set asset_count and asset_ids
aggregated_orphans AS (
  SELECT 'orphan:' || owner AS group_key,
         ARRAY[]::integer[] AS ids,
         ARRAY[]::text[] AS usernames,
         ARRAY[]::text[] AS refs1,
         ARRAY[owner]::text[] AS refs2,
         COUNT(DISTINCT asset_id) AS asset_count,
         ARRAY_AGG(DISTINCT asset_id ORDER BY asset_id) AS asset_ids,
         MIN(asset_id) AS min_asset_id
  FROM orphan_assets
  GROUP BY owner
)
SELECT
  ids,
  usernames,
  refs1,
  refs2,
  asset_count,
  asset_ids
FROM (
  SELECT group_key, ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids, min_asset_id
  FROM aggregated_groups
  UNION ALL
  SELECT group_key, ids, usernames, refs1, refs2, asset_count, asset_ids, min_asset_id
  FROM aggregated_orphans
) z
ORDER BY min_asset_id;
